general.g_text: 일반적으로 일은 놀이와 대조된다. 놀이는 목적이 놀이 그 자체지만, 일은 목적을 달성하기 위한 활동이다. 그래서 같은 행동이라도 놀이로 하느냐 일로 하느냐에 따라 그때의 심리 상태가 달라진다. 노는 것이 목적인 놀이에서는 즐거움을 느끼지만 목적을 이루기 위해 하는 일에서는 (        ) 생각이 들게 된다. 일을 하는 과정에서 수반되는 고통을 이겨 내고 일을 즐겁게 하려면 삶을 긍정적으로 바라보는 태도가 필요하다. 결과가 아니라 일 그 자체에서 의의를 발견한다면 일을 놀이처럼 할 수 있을 것이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Thông thường công việc sẽ đối lập với vui chơi. Vui chơi chỉ có mục đích đơn thuần là vui chơi, nhưng công việc là hoạt động để đạt được mục đích. Vậy nên cùng một hoạt động nhưng tùy theo việc xem đó là vui chơi hay công việc mà trạng thái tâm lý lúc đó sẽ khác nhau. Trong hoạt động vui chơi lấy việc chơi làm mục đích thì chúng ta sẽ thấy vui vẻ, còn trong công việc làm để đạt mục đích thì chúng ta sẽ có suy nghĩ  (        ). Nếu muốn chiến thắng nỗi khổ bị kéo theo trong quá trình làm việc và làm một cách vui vẻ thì cần có thái độ nhìn cuộc sống một cách tích cực. Nếu chúng ta phát hiện ra ý nghĩa trong bản thân công việc đó chứ không phải là trong kết quả thì chúng ta có thể làm việc như vui chơi.
g_text_translate.en: In general, work is contrasted with play. While play has an end in itself, work is an activity to achieve an end. Therefore, even if the same behavior is performed as a play or work, the psychological state at that time changes. In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. In order to overcome the pain that accompanies the process of work and to enjoy work, it is necessary to have a positive attitude to life. If you find meaning in the work itself, not the result, you can make work like a game.
g_text_translate.fr:
En général, le travail s'oppose au jeu. Alors que le jeu a une fin en soi, le travail est une activité pour atteindre une fin. Par conséquent, même si le même comportement est exécuté comme jeu ou travail, l'état psychologique à ce moment-là change. Dans un jeu dont le but est de jouer, nous ressentons du plaisir, mais dans le travail que nous faisons pour atteindre le but, nous nous sentons douloureux et peu disposés à le faire. Afin de surmonter la douleur qui accompagne le processus de travail et d'apprécier le travail, il est nécessaire d'avoir une attitude positive face à la vie. Si vous trouvez un sens dans le travail lui-même, pas dans le résultat, vous pouvez faire en sorte que le travail ressemble à un jeu.

#######

In general, work is contrasted with play. While play has an end in itself, work is an activity to achieve an end. Therefore, even if the same behavior is performed as a play or work, the psychological state at that time changes. In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. In order to overcome the pain that accompanies the process of work and to enjoy work, it is necessary to have a positive attitude to life. If you find meaning in the work itself, not the result, you can make work like a game.
g_text_translate.es:
En general, el trabajo se contrasta con el juego. Mientras que el juego tiene un fin en sí mismo, el trabajo es una actividad para lograr un fin. Por lo tanto, incluso si el mismo comportamiento se realiza como un juego o una obra, el estado psicológico en ese momento cambia. En un juego cuyo propósito es jugar, sentimos placer, pero en el trabajo que hacemos para lograr el propósito, sentimos dolor y falta de voluntad para hacerlo. Para superar el dolor que acompaña el proceso de trabajo y disfrutar del trabajo, es necesario tener una actitud positiva ante la vida. Si encuentra significado en el trabajo en sí, no en el resultado, puede hacer que el trabajo sea como un juego.

#######

In general, work is contrasted with play. While play has an end in itself, work is an activity to achieve an end. Therefore, even if the same behavior is performed as a play or work, the psychological state at that time changes. In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. In order to overcome the pain that accompanies the process of work and to enjoy work, it is necessary to have a positive attitude to life. If you find meaning in the work itself, not the result, you can make work like a game.
g_text_translate.id:
Secara umum, bekerja dikontraskan dengan bermain. Sementara bermain memiliki tujuan itu sendiri, bekerja adalah kegiatan untuk mencapai tujuan. Oleh karena itu, bahkan jika perilaku yang sama dilakukan sebagai permainan atau pekerjaan, keadaan psikologis pada saat itu berubah. Dalam permainan yang tujuannya untuk bermain, kita merasakan kesenangan, tetapi dalam pekerjaan yang kita lakukan untuk mencapai tujuan, kita merasa sakit dan tidak mau melakukannya. Untuk mengatasi rasa sakit yang menyertai proses kerja dan untuk menikmati pekerjaan, diperlukan sikap positif terhadap kehidupan. Jika Anda menemukan makna dalam pekerjaan itu sendiri, bukan hasilnya, Anda dapat membuat pekerjaan seperti permainan.

#######

In general, work is contrasted with play. While play has an end in itself, work is an activity to achieve an end. Therefore, even if the same behavior is performed as a play or work, the psychological state at that time changes. In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. In order to overcome the pain that accompanies the process of work and to enjoy work, it is necessary to have a positive attitude to life. If you find meaning in the work itself, not the result, you can make work like a game.
g_text_translate.ja:
一般的に、仕事は遊びと対比されます。遊びはそれ自体に目的がありますが、仕事は目的を達成するための活動です。そのため、同じ行為を芝居や作品として行っても、その時の心理状態は変化します。私たちは、遊ぶことが目的のゲームでは喜びを感じますが、目的を達成するために行う作業では、苦痛を感じ、やりたくないと感じます。仕事に伴う苦痛を乗り越え、仕事を楽しむためには、前向きに生きることが必要です。結果ではなく、作品そのものに意味を見出せば、作品をゲームのように作ることができます。

#######

In general, work is contrasted with play. While play has an end in itself, work is an activity to achieve an end. Therefore, even if the same behavior is performed as a play or work, the psychological state at that time changes. In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. In order to overcome the pain that accompanies the process of work and to enjoy work, it is necessary to have a positive attitude to life. If you find meaning in the work itself, not the result, you can make work like a game.
g_text_translate.de:
Im Allgemeinen wird Arbeit dem Spiel gegenübergestellt. Während das Spiel einen Zweck an sich hat, ist die Arbeit eine Aktivität, um ein Ziel zu erreichen. Daher ändert sich der psychologische Zustand zu diesem Zeitpunkt, selbst wenn dasselbe Verhalten als Spiel oder Arbeit ausgeführt wird. In einem Spiel, dessen Zweck das Spielen ist, empfinden wir Vergnügen, aber in der Arbeit, die wir leisten, um den Zweck zu erreichen, fühlen wir uns schmerzhaft und nicht bereit, es zu tun. Um den mit dem Arbeitsprozess einhergehenden Schmerz zu überwinden und Freude an der Arbeit zu haben, ist eine positive Lebenseinstellung notwendig. Wenn Sie in der Arbeit selbst einen Sinn finden und nicht im Ergebnis, können Sie die Arbeit wie ein Spiel gestalten.

#######

In general, work is contrasted with play. While play has an end in itself, work is an activity to achieve an end. Therefore, even if the same behavior is performed as a play or work, the psychological state at that time changes. In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. In order to overcome the pain that accompanies the process of work and to enjoy work, it is necessary to have a positive attitude to life. If you find meaning in the work itself, not the result, you can make work like a game.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
一般来说，工作与游戏相对。虽然游戏本身有目的，但工作是一种达到目的的活动。因此，即使将相同的行为作为戏剧或作品进行，当时的心理状态也会发生变化。在一个以玩为目的的游戏中，我们感到快乐，但在我们为达到目的所做的工作中，我们感到痛苦并且不愿意去做。要想克服工作过程中的痛苦，享受工作，就必须要有积极的人生态度。如果你在作品本身而不是结果中找到意义，你就可以把作品当成游戏。

#######

In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. In order to overcome the pain that accompanies the process of work and to enjoy work, it is necessary to have a positive attitude to life. If you find meaning in the work itself, not the result, you can make work like a game.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
一般來說，工作與遊戲相對。雖然遊戲本身有目的，但工作是一種達到目的的活動。因此，即使將相同的行為作為戲劇或作品進行，當時的心理狀態也會發生變化。在一個以玩為目的的遊戲中，我們感到快樂，但在我們為達到目的所做的工作中，我們感到痛苦並且不願意去做。要想克服工作過程中的痛苦，享受工作，就必須要有積極的人生態度。如果你在作品本身而不是結果中找到意義，你就可以把作品當成遊戲。

g_text_translate.it:
In generale, il lavoro è in contrasto con il gioco. Mentre il gioco ha fine a se stesso, il lavoro è un'attività per raggiungere un fine. Pertanto, anche se lo stesso comportamento viene eseguito come gioco o lavoro, lo stato psicologico in quel momento cambia. In un gioco il cui scopo è giocare, proviamo piacere, ma nel lavoro che facciamo per raggiungere lo scopo, ci sentiamo dolorosi e riluttanti a farlo. Per superare il dolore che accompagna il processo lavorativo e godersi il lavoro, è necessario avere un atteggiamento positivo nei confronti della vita. Se trovi un significato nel lavoro stesso, non nel risultato, puoi rendere il lavoro come un gioco.

#######

In general, work is contrasted with play. While play has an end in itself, work is an activity to achieve an end. Therefore, even if the same behavior is performed as a play or work, the psychological state at that time changes. In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. g_text_translate.ru:
В целом работа противопоставляется игре. Хотя игра имеет цель сама по себе, работа — это деятельность, направленная на достижение цели. Поэтому, даже если одно и то же поведение совершается в виде игры или произведения, психологическое состояние в это время меняется. В игре, целью которой является игра, мы испытываем удовольствие, но в работе, которую мы выполняем для достижения цели, мы чувствуем боль и не хотим ее делать. Чтобы преодолеть боль, сопровождающую процесс труда, и получать удовольствие от работы, необходимо позитивно относиться к жизни. Если вы находите смысл в самой работе, а не в результате, вы можете превратить работу в игру.

g_text_translate.th:
โดยทั่วไปแล้ว งานย่อมตรงกันข้ามกับการเล่น แม้ว่าการเล่นจะมีจุดสิ้นสุดในตัวมันเอง แต่งานก็เป็นกิจกรรมที่มุ่งไปสู่จุดสิ้นสุด ดังนั้นแม้ว่าจะมีการแสดงพฤติกรรมเดียวกันในการเล่นหรือการทำงาน แต่สภาพจิตใจในขณะนั้นก็เปลี่ยนไป ในเกมที่มีจุดประสงค์ในการเล่น เรารู้สึกมีความสุข แต่ในงานที่เราทำเพื่อให้บรรลุวัตถุประสงค์ เรารู้สึกเจ็บปวดและไม่เต็มใจที่จะทำมัน เพื่อที่จะเอาชนะความเจ็บปวดที่มาพร้อมกับกระบวนการทำงานและสนุกกับงานได้ จำเป็นต้องมีทัศนคติเชิงบวกต่อชีวิต หากคุณพบความหมายในตัวงาน ไม่ใช่ผลลัพธ์ คุณสามารถทำงานให้เป็นเหมือนเกมได้

g_text_translate.pt:
Em geral, o trabalho é contrastado com a diversão. Embora a brincadeira tenha um fim em si, o trabalho é uma atividade para atingir um fim. Portanto, mesmo que o mesmo comportamento seja realizado como uma brincadeira ou um trabalho, o estado psicológico naquele momento muda. Num jogo cujo objetivo é jogar, sentimos prazer, mas no trabalho que fazemos para atingir o objetivo, sentimos dor e falta de vontade de fazê-lo. Para superar a dor que acompanha o processo de trabalho e gostar do trabalho, é necessário ter uma atitude positiva perante a vida. Se você encontrar significado no trabalho em si, e não no resultado, poderá transformar o trabalho em um jogo.

#######

In general, work is contrasted with play. While play has an end in itself, work is an activity to achieve an end. Therefore, even if the same behavior is performed as a play or work, the psychological state at that time changes. In a game whose purpose is to play, we feel pleasure, but in the work we do to achieve the purpose, we feel painful and unwilling to do it. In order to overcome the pain that accompanies the process of work and to enjoy work, it is necessary to have a positive attitude to life. If you find meaning in the work itself, not the result, you can make work like a game.
g_text_translate.hi:
सामान्य तौर पर, काम की तुलना खेल से की जाती है। जबकि खेल का अपने आप में एक अंत है, काम एक लक्ष्य को प्राप्त करने की गतिविधि है। इसलिए यदि वही व्यवहार किसी खेल या कार्य के रूप में भी किया जाए तो उस समय की मनोवैज्ञानिक स्थिति बदल जाती है। जिस खेल को खेलने का उद्देश्य होता है, उसमें हमें आनंद की अनुभूति होती है, लेकिन उस उद्देश्य की प्राप्ति के लिए हम जो काम करते हैं, उसमें हमें दुख होता है और उसे करने में अनिच्छा होती है। काम की प्रक्रिया के साथ होने वाले दर्द को दूर करने और काम का आनंद लेने के लिए जीवन के प्रति सकारात्मक दृष्टिकोण रखना आवश्यक है। यदि आप कार्य में ही अर्थ ढूंढते हैं, न कि परिणाम में, तो आप कार्य को एक खेल की तरह बना सकते हैं।

g_text_translate.tr:
Genel olarak iş oyunla tezat oluşturuyor. Oyunun başlı başına bir amacı varken, çalışmak bir amaca ulaşma etkinliğidir. Dolayısıyla aynı davranış oyun veya iş olarak yapılsa bile o andaki psikolojik durum değişir. Amacı oynamak olan bir oyunda zevk duyarız ama amaca ulaşmak için yaptığımız işte acı çekeriz ve bunu yapmak istemeziz. Çalışma sürecine eşlik eden acının üstesinden gelmek ve işten keyif almak için hayata karşı olumlu bir tutuma sahip olmak gerekir. Sonuçta değil, işin kendisinde anlam bulursanız işi oyun gibi hale getirebilirsiniz.

#######

content.q_text: 이 글의 중심 생각을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 긍정적인 태도로 사는 사람은 목적을 쉽게 달성할 수 있다.
일을 하는 과정에서 의의를 찾아야 일을 기쁘게 할 수 있다.
놀이와 일은 목적에 따라 다른 것이므로 이를 구분해야 한다.
즐거운 놀이를 통해 일을 하면 수반되는 고통을 줄일 수 있다.

explain.vi:
Chọn suy nghĩ trọng tâm của bài viết.
1. Người sống với thái độ tích cực có thể dễ dàng đạt được mục đích.
2. Phải tìm ra ý nghĩa trong quá trình làm việc mới có thể làm việc một cách vui vẻ.
3. Vui chơi và công việc khác nhau tùy theo mục đích nên phải phân biệt chúng.
4. Nhờ vui chơi mà có thể giảm được nỗi khổ bị kéo theo khi làm việc.
--------------------
의의: Ý nghĩa
자체: Bản thân, tự thế

V + 아/어야: Phải làm gì đó thì mới có nội dung phía sau.

Bài viết nói về sự khác nhau khi vui chơi và làm việc và kết luận rằng cần tìm ra ý nghĩa trong bản thân công việc đó thì mới có thể làm việc như vui chơi.
explain.en:
Choose the main thought of the paragraph.
1. People who live with a positive attitude can easily achieve their goals.
2. Must find meaning in the process of working to be able to work happily.
3. Play and work are different according to the purpose, so they must be distinguished.
4. By having fun, it is possible to reduce the suffering involved in working.
--------------------
<g>의의:</g> Meaning
<g>자체:</g> Oneself

<g>V + 아/어야:</g> Have to do A, so B can.

The paragraph talks about the difference between play and work and concludes that it is necessary to find meaning in the work itself in order to work as play.
explain.fr:
Choisissez l'idée principale du paragraphe.
1. Les personnes qui vivent avec une attitude positive peuvent facilement atteindre leurs objectifs.
2. Doit trouver un sens dans le processus de travail pour pouvoir travailler heureux.
3. Le jeu et le travail sont différents selon le but, il faut donc les distinguer.
4. En s'amusant, il est possible de réduire la souffrance liée au travail.
--------------------
<g>의의:</g>Sens
<g>자체:</g>Soi-même

<g>V + 아/어야:</g>Je dois faire A, donc B peut.

Le paragraphe parle de la différence entre le jeu et le travail et conclut qu'il est nécessaire de trouver un sens dans le travail lui-même pour travailler comme un jeu.

explain.es:
Elige el pensamiento principal del párrafo.
1. Las personas que viven con una actitud positiva pueden lograr fácilmente sus objetivos.
2. Debe encontrar sentido en el proceso de trabajo para poder trabajar felizmente.

explain.vi:
Chọn câu đúng để điền vào phần (        ).
1. Mệt mỏi và ghét làm
2. Trò chơi sớm trở thành công việc
3. Phải vui chơi như làm việc
4. Làm cho người khác vui
--------------------
놀이: Chơi
즐거움: Niềm vui
일: Công việc
괴롭다: Mệt mỏi
싫다: Ghét

Tùy theo việc nhìn nhận một hành động là vui chơi hay công việc mà trạng thái tâm lý sẽ khác nhau. Ở vế trước đã nói rằng khi coi đó là chơi thì sẽ cảm nhận niềm vui, vậy đối với công việc sẽ ngược lại. Hai về nối với nhau bằng "-지만" (nhưng) thể hiện ý nghĩa đối lập.
explain.en:
Choose the correct one to fill in ( ).
1. Painful and unwilling to do it.
2. That play becomes work.
3. We should do it like work.
4. That we please others.
--------------------
<g>놀이:</g> Play, game
<g>즐거움:</g> Joy, pleasure
<g>일:</g> Work
<g>괴롭다:</g> To be painful
<g>싫다:</g> To hate

Depending on whether an action is considered play or work, the psychological state will be different. In the previous sentence, it was said that when you consider it a play, you will feel joy, so for work, it will be the opposite. The two terms are joined by "-지만" (but) expressing opposite meanings.
explain.fr:
Choisissez le bon à remplir ( ).
1. Douloureux et peu disposé à le faire.
2. Que le jeu devient un travail.
3. Nous devrions le faire comme un travail.
4. Que nous plaisons aux autres.
--------------------
<g>놀이:</g>Jouer, jeu
<g>즐거움:</g>Joie, plaisir
<g>일:</g>Travailler
<g>괴롭다:</g>Être douloureux
<g>싫다:</g>Détester

Selon qu'une action est considérée comme un jeu ou un travail, l'état psychologique sera différent. Dans la phrase précédente, il était dit que lorsque vous considérez cela comme une pièce de théâtre, vous ressentirez de la joie, alors pour le travail, ce sera le contraire. Les deux termes sont joints par "-지만" (mais) exprimant des sens opposés.

<g>일:</g>Trabajar
<g>괴롭다:</g>ser doloroso
<g>싫다:</g>Odiar

Dependiendo de si una acción se considera juego o trabajo, el estado psicológico será diferente. En la oración anterior se dijo que cuando lo consideres un juego, sentirás alegría, por lo que para el trabajo será todo lo contrario. Los dos términos están unidos por "-지만" (pero) expresando significados opuestos.

<g>괴롭다:</g>痛むこと
<g>싫다:</g>嫌いに

行動が遊びか仕事かによって、心理状態は異なります。前の文で、遊びと考えると喜びを感じると言いましたが、仕事は逆になります。 2 つの用語は、反対の意味を表す「-지만 (しかし)」で結合されます。

#######

Choose the correct one to fill in ( ).
1. Painful and unwilling to do it.
2. That play becomes work.
3. We should do it like work.
4. That we please others.
--------------------
<g>놀이:</g> Play, game
<g>즐거움:</g> Joy, pleasure
<g>일:</g> Work
<g>괴롭다:</g> To be painful
<g>싫다:</g> To hate

Depending on whether an action is considered play or work, the psychological state will be different. In the previous sentence, it was said that when you consider it a play, you will feel joy, so for work, it will be the opposite. The two terms are joined by "-지만" (but) expressing opposite meanings.

<g>일:</g>Arbeit

<g>싫다:</g>Hassen

general.g_text: 딸의 다섯 번째 생일을 앞두고 나는 별다른 뜻 없이 지윤이에게 생일날에 제일 하고 싶은 게 뭐냐고 물었다. 그런데 한참을 생각하던 지윤이의 대답은 외할머니한테 가자는 것이었다. 의외의 대답에 깜짝 놀란 내가 그 이유를 묻자 “엄마가 지윤이를 낳았고, 엄마를 할머니가 낳아 주셨으니까 할머니한테 절하려고요.” 하는 것이 아닌가. <u>그 순간 나는 잠시 할 말을 잃었다</u>. 정작 나를 낳아 준 엄마를 까맣게 잊고 있던 내 자신이 떠올랐기 때문이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Sắp đến sinh nhật 5 tuổi của con gái, tôi hỏi Ji Yoon xe con bé muốn làm gì trong ngày sinh nhật nhất chứ không có ý gì đặc biệt. Nhưng sau một lúc lâu suy nghĩ, câu trả lời của Ji Yoon là hãy đi đến với bà ngoại. Tôi giật mình trước câu trả lời bất ngờ này, tôi hỏi lý do thì con bảo "Mẹ sinh ra Ji Yoon, còn bà ngoại sinh ra mẹ, nên Ji Yoon muốn đến lạy bà." <u> Vào lúc đó tôi nghẹn lời trong phút chốc </u>. Vì tôi nhận ra mình đã thực sự quên bằng đi người mẹ đã sinh ra mình.
g_text_translate.en: On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.fr:
La veille du cinquième anniversaire de ma fille, j'ai demandé à Jiyoon ce qu'elle voulait le plus faire pour son anniversaire sans trop réfléchir. Mais la réponse de Jiyoon après y avoir longuement réfléchi a été d'aller chez sa grand-mère. Quand j'ai demandé pourquoi, surprise par la réponse inattendue, elle a dit: "Tu m'as donné naissance, et ma grand-mère t'a donné naissance, alors je vais m'incliner devant ma grand-mère." <u>À ce moment, j'étais sans voix</u>. C'est parce que je me suis souvenu de moi, qui avait complètement oublié ma mère qui m'a mis au monde.

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.es:
En la víspera del quinto cumpleaños de mi hija, le pregunté a Jiyoon qué era lo que más quería hacer en su cumpleaños sin pensarlo mucho. Pero la respuesta de Jiyoon después de pensarlo durante mucho tiempo fue ir con su abuela. Cuando le pregunté por qué, sorprendida por la respuesta inesperada, dijo: "Tú me diste a luz y mi abuela te dio a luz, así que me inclinaré ante mi abuela". <u>En ese momento, me quedé sin palabras</u>. Fue porque me recordé a mí mismo, que había olvidado por completo a mi madre que me dio a luz.

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.id:
Pada malam ulang tahun kelima putriku, aku bertanya pada Jiyoon apa yang paling ingin dia lakukan di hari ulang tahunnya tanpa banyak berpikir. Tapi jawaban Jiyoon setelah memikirkannya lama adalah pergi ke neneknya. Ketika saya bertanya mengapa, terkejut dengan jawaban yang tidak terduga, dia berkata, "Kamu melahirkan saya, dan nenek saya melahirkan kamu, jadi saya akan sujud kepada nenek saya." <u>Pada saat itu, saya terdiam</u>. Itu karena saya ingat diri saya sendiri, yang telah benar-benar melupakan ibu saya yang melahirkan saya.

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.ja:
娘の5歳の誕生日の前夜、ジユンに誕生日に一番やりたいことは何なのか、あまり考えずに聞いてみた。しかし、ジユンが長い間考えた末の答えは祖母に行くことだった。その理由を尋ねると、意外な答えに驚いて、「あなたは私を産み、祖母はあなたを産んだのだから、祖母に頭を下げます」と言いました。 <u>その瞬間、私は言葉を失いました</u>。それは、私を産んでくれた母のことをすっかり忘れていた自分を思い出したからです。

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.de:
Am Vorabend des fünften Geburtstags meiner Tochter fragte ich Jiyoon ohne viel nachzudenken, was sie an ihrem Geburtstag am liebsten machen würde. Aber Jiyoons Antwort, nachdem sie lange darüber nachgedacht hatte, war, zu ihrer Großmutter zu gehen. Als ich nach dem Grund fragte, sagte sie überrascht von der unerwarteten Antwort: "Du hast mich geboren, und meine Großmutter hat dich geboren, also werde ich mich vor meiner Großmutter verneigen." <u>In diesem Moment war ich sprachlos</u>. Es war, weil ich mich an mich selbst erinnerte, der meine Mutter, die mich geboren hatte, völlig vergessen hatte.

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
在女儿五岁生日的前夕，我不假思索地问智允她生日最想做的事情是什么。但智允想了很久的回答是去找奶奶。当我问为什么，她对意外的回答感到惊讶，她说：“你生了我，我的祖母生了你，所以我要向我的祖母鞠躬。” <u>那一刻，我无言以对</u>。因为我想起了自己，完全忘记了生我的母亲。

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
在女兒五歲生日的前夕，我不假思索地問智允她生日最想做的事情是什麼。但智允想了很久的回答是去找奶奶。當我問為什麼，她對意外的回答感到驚訝，她說：“你生了我，我的祖母生了你，所以我要向我的祖母鞠躬。” <u>那一刻，我無言以對</u>。因為我想起了自己，完全忘記了生我的母親。

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.it:
Alla vigilia del quinto compleanno di mia figlia, ho chiesto a Jiyoon cosa desiderava fare di più per il suo compleanno senza pensarci troppo. Ma la risposta di Jiyoon dopo averci pensato a lungo è stata di andare da sua nonna. Quando ho chiesto perché, sorpresa dalla risposta inaspettata, ha detto: "Mi hai dato alla luce e mia nonna ti ha partorito, quindi mi inchinerò davanti a mia nonna". <u>In quel momento sono rimasto senza parole</u>. Era perché mi ricordavo di me stesso, che avevo completamente dimenticato mia madre che mi aveva partorito.

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.ru:
Накануне пятого дня рождения дочери я, не задумываясь, спросил Джиюн, чем она больше всего хочет заняться в свой день рождения. Но ответ Джиюн, после долгих раздумий, заключался в том, чтобы пойти к бабушке. Когда я спросила, почему, удивившись неожиданному ответу, она сказала: «Ты меня родила, а моя бабушка родила тебя, поэтому я собираюсь поклониться бабушке». <u>В этот момент я потерял дар речи</u>. Потому что я вспомнил себя, совершенно забывшего свою мать, родившую меня.

#######

But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. g_text_translate.th:
ก่อนวันเกิดปีที่ 5 ของลูกสาวฉัน ฉันถามจียุนว่าเธออยากทำอะไรมากที่สุดในวันเกิดโดยไม่ต้องคิดมาก แต่คำตอบของจียุนหลังจากคิดอยู่นานก็คือไปหาย่าของเธอ เมื่อฉันถามว่าทำไม เธอถึงประหลาดใจกับคำตอบที่ไม่คาดคิด เธอตอบว่า "คุณให้กำเนิดฉัน และคุณย่าของฉันก็ให้กำเนิดคุณ ดังนั้น ฉันจะกราบไหว้คุณย่าของฉัน" <u>ในขณะนั้น ฉันก็พูดไม่ออก</u> เป็นเพราะฉันจำตัวเองที่ลืมแม่ที่ให้กำเนิดฉันไปหมดแล้ว

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. g_text_translate.pt:
Na véspera do quinto aniversário da minha filha, perguntei a Jiyoon o que ela mais queria fazer no aniversário sem pensar muito. Mas a resposta de Jiyoon depois de pensar muito sobre isso foi ir até sua avó. Quando perguntei por que, surpresa com a resposta inesperada, ela disse: “Você me deu à luz e minha avó deu à luz você, então vou me curvar diante de minha avó”. <u>Naquele momento, fiquei sem palavras</u>. Foi porque me lembrei de mim mesmo, que havia esquecido completamente minha mãe que me deu à luz.

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.hi:
अपनी बेटी के पांचवें जन्मदिन की पूर्व संध्या पर, मैंने बिना ज्यादा सोचे-समझे जियून से पूछा कि वह अपने जन्मदिन पर सबसे ज्यादा क्या करना चाहती है। लेकिन काफी देर तक सोचने के बाद जियून का जवाब था कि उसे अपनी दादी के पास जाना है। जब मैंने पूछा कि क्यों, अप्रत्याशित उत्तर से आश्चर्यचकित होकर, उसने कहा, "आपने मुझे जन्म दिया, और मेरी दादी ने आपको जन्म दिया, इसलिए मैं अपनी दादी को प्रणाम करने जा रही हूं।" <u>उस पल, मैं अवाक रह गया</u>। ऐसा इसलिए था क्योंकि मुझे खुद की याद आ गई थी, मैं अपनी उस मां को पूरी तरह से भूल गई थी जिसने मुझे जन्म दिया था।

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.
g_text_translate.tr:
Kızımın beşinci yaş gününün arifesinde, Jiyoon'a doğum gününde çok fazla düşünmeden en çok ne yapmak istediğini sordum. Ancak Jiyoon'un uzun süre düşündükten sonra verdiği cevap büyükannesinin yanına gitmek oldu. Nedenini sorduğumda beklenmedik cevap karşısında şaşırarak, "Beni sen doğurdun, büyükannem de seni doğurdu, o yüzden ben de büyükannemin önünde eğileceğim" dedi. <u>O anda suskundum</u>. Çünkü beni doğuran annemi tamamen unutmuş olan kendimi hatırladım.

#######

On the eve of my daughter's fifth birthday, I asked Jiyoon what she wanted to do the most on her birthday without much thought. But Jiyoon's answer after thinking about it for a long time was to go to her grandmother. When I asked why, surprised by the unexpected answer, she said, "You gave birth to me, and my grandmother gave birth to you, so I'm going to bow down to my grandmother." <u>At that moment, I was speechless</u>. It was because I remembered myself, who had completely forgotten my mother who gave birth to me.

content.q_text: 이 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 나는 엄마의 생일이 언제인지 잊어버렸다.
나는 지윤이의 속마음까지도 잘 알고 있었다.
지윤이는 생일에 외할머니가 집에 오셨으면 한다.
지윤이는 외할머니가 엄마를 낳아 준 것을 고마워한다.

explain.vi:
Chọn câu giống với nội dung trên.
1. Tôi quên mất sinh nhật mẹ là khi nào.
2. Tôi biết rõ tận đáy lòng Ji Yoon.
3. Ji Yoon muốn bà đến nhà vào ngày sinh nhật.
4. Ji Yoon biết ơn bà đã sinh ra mẹ.
--------------------
절하다: Lạy

Ji Yoon nói vì bà sinh ra mẹ nên muốn lạy bà, theo văn hóa Hàn Quốc là bày tỏ sự biết ơn và kính trọng.
explain.en:
Choose one that matches the content of this paragraph.
1. I forgot when my mother's birthday is.
2. I was well aware of Jiyoon's inner thoughts.
3. Ji Yoon wants her grandmother to come to her house on her birthday.
4. Ji Yoon is grateful to her grandmother for giving birth to her mother.
--------------------
<g>절하다:</g> To bow down

Ji Yoon said that because her grandmother gave birth to her mother, so she wanted to bow down in front of her grandmother. In Korean culture it is to express gratitude and respect.
explain.fr:
Choisissez-en un qui correspond au contenu de ce paragraphe.
1. J'ai oublié quand est l'anniversaire de ma mère.
2. J'étais bien conscient des pensées intérieures de Jiyoon.
3. Ji Yoon veut que sa grand-mère vienne chez elle le jour de son anniversaire.
4. Ji Yoon est reconnaissante envers sa grand-mère d'avoir donné naissance à sa mère.
--------------------
<g>절하다:</g>Se prosterner

Ji Yoon a déclaré que parce que sa grand-mère avait donné naissance à sa mère, elle voulait donc se prosterner devant sa grand-mère. Dans la culture coréenne, il s'agit d'exprimer sa gratitude et son respect.

general.g_text: 디지털 치매는 디지털 기기에 의존하면서 기억하고 있던 내용이 갑자기 떠오르지 않는 현상을 말한다. 계산기 없이는 계산이 안 된다든지, 노래방 화면의 가사를 보지 않으면 노래를 못 부른다든지 하는 경우가 대표적이다. 이런 상황이 계속 반복된다면 생활하는 데 불편이 따를 뿐만 아니라 학습이나 업무 능력도 서서히 떨어질 수 있기 때문에 가볍게 여길 일만은 아니다. 디지털 치매를 예방하기 위해서는 되도록 (        ) 일을 하는 것이 좋다. 가까운 사람에게 전화할 때는 단축 버튼을 사용하지 않고 번호를 직접 누른다거나, 일기도 직접 종이에 쓰는 것이 도움이 된다.

g_text_translate.vi: Đãng trí điện tử là hiện tượng lệ thuộc vào thiết bị điện tử và đột nhiên không nghĩ ra những nội dung mình đang ghi nhớ. Tiêu biểu trường hợp không thể tính toán khi không có máy tính hoặc không thể hát nếu không nhìn vào lời bài hát trên màn hình karaoke. Nếu hiện tượng này lặp lại liên tục thì nó không chỉ gây bất tiện trong cuộc sống mà năng lực học tập hay làm việc cũng dần dần giảm đi, nên đây không phải là một việc có thể xem nhẹ. Để phòng chống đãng trí điện tử, nên làm việc  (        ). Khi gọi điện cho những người gần gũi, đừng dùng phím tắt mà trực tiếp nhấn số, hay viết nhật kí trực tiếp lên giấy sẽ có ích.
g_text_translate.en: Digital dementia refers to a phenomenon in which memories suddenly do not come to mind while relying on digital devices. For example, if you can't calculate without a calculator, or if you don't see the lyrics on the karaoke screen, you can't sing. If this situation repeats over and over again, it is not something to be taken lightly, because not only will it be difficult to live, but also learning or work ability may gradually decline. In order to prevent digital dementia, it is better to do something that directly stimulates the brain as much as possible. When calling someone close to you, it is helpful to dial the number directly without using the speed button, or to write your diary on paper.
g_text_translate.fr:
La démence numérique fait référence à un phénomène dans lequel les souvenirs ne viennent soudainement pas à l'esprit en s'appuyant sur des appareils numériques. Par exemple, si vous ne pouvez pas calculer sans calculatrice, ou si vous ne voyez pas les paroles sur l'écran de karaoké, vous ne pouvez pas chanter. Si cette situation se répète encore et encore, ce n'est pas quelque chose à prendre à la légère, car non seulement elle sera difficile à vivre, mais en plus la capacité d'apprentissage ou de travail risque de décliner progressivement. Afin de prévenir la démence numérique, il est préférable de faire quelque chose qui stimule le plus possible le cerveau directement. Lorsque vous appelez un proche, il est utile de composer directement le numéro sans utiliser le bouton abrégé, ou d'écrire votre agenda sur papier.

#######

Digital dementia refers to a phenomenon in which memories suddenly do not come to mind while relying on digital devices. For example, if you can't calculate without a calculator, or if you don't see the lyrics on the karaoke screen, you can't sing. If this situation repeats over and over again, it is not something to be taken lightly, because not only will it be difficult to live, but also learning or work ability may gradually decline. In order to prevent digital dementia, it is better to do something that directly stimulates the brain as much as possible. When calling someone close to you, it is helpful to dial the number directly without using the speed button, or to write your diary on paper.
g_text_translate.es:
La demencia digital se refiere a un fenómeno en el que los recuerdos de repente no vienen a la mente al depender de dispositivos digitales. Por ejemplo, si no puede calcular sin una calculadora, o si no ve la letra en la pantalla del karaoke, no puede cantar. Si esta situación se repite una y otra vez, no es algo que deba tomarse a la ligera, ya que no solo será difícil vivir, sino que la capacidad de aprendizaje o trabajo puede disminuir gradualmente. Para prevenir la demencia digital, es mejor hacer algo que estimule directamente el cerebro tanto como sea posible. Cuando llame a alguien cercano a usted, es útil marcar el número directamente sin usar el botón de velocidad o escribir su diario en un papel.

#######

Digital dementia refers to a phenomenon in which memories suddenly do not come to mind while relying on digital devices. For example, if you can't calculate without a calculator, or if you don't see the lyrics on the karaoke screen, you can't sing. If this situation repeats over and over again, it is not something to be taken lightly, because not only will it be difficult to live, but also learning or work ability may gradually decline. In order to prevent digital dementia, it is better to do something that directly stimulates the brain as much as possible. When calling someone close to you, it is helpful to dial the number directly without using the speed button, or to write your diary on paper.
g_text_translate.id:
Demensia digital mengacu pada fenomena di mana ingatan tiba-tiba tidak muncul di benak saat mengandalkan perangkat digital. Misalnya, jika Anda tidak dapat menghitung tanpa kalkulator, atau jika Anda tidak melihat lirik di layar karaoke, Anda tidak dapat bernyanyi. Jika situasi ini berulang-ulang, itu bukan sesuatu yang bisa dianggap enteng, karena bukan hanya akan sulit untuk hidup, tetapi juga kemampuan belajar atau bekerja dapat menurun secara bertahap. Untuk mencegah demensia digital, lebih baik melakukan sesuatu yang secara langsung merangsang otak sebanyak mungkin. Saat menelepon seseorang yang dekat dengan Anda, akan sangat membantu untuk menghubungi nomor tersebut secara langsung tanpa menggunakan tombol kecepatan, atau menulis buku harian Anda di atas kertas.

#######

Digital dementia refers to a phenomenon in which memories suddenly do not come to mind while relying on digital devices. For example, if you can't calculate without a calculator, or if you don't see the lyrics on the karaoke screen, you can't sing. g_text_translate.ja:
デジタル認知症とは、デジタル機器に頼っていると突然記憶が思い浮かばなくなる現象を指します。例えば、電卓がないと計算できないとか、カラオケの画面で歌詞が見えないと歌えません。このような状況が繰り返されると、生活が困難になるだけでなく、学習や仕事の能力が徐々に低下する可能性があるため、軽視することはできません。デジタル認知症を予防するためには、できるだけ脳に直接刺激を与えるようなことをしたほうがよいでしょう。身近な人に電話をかけるときは、短縮ボタンを使わずに直接ダイヤルするか、日記を紙に書いておくと便利です。

#######

Digital dementia refers to a phenomenon in which memories suddenly do not come to mind while relying on digital devices. For example, if you can't calculate without a calculator, or if you don't see the lyrics on the karaoke screen, you can't sing. If this situation repeats over and over again, it is not something to be taken lightly, because not only will it be difficult to live, but also learning or work ability may gradually decline. In order to prevent digital dementia, it is better to do something that directly stimulates the brain as much as possible. When calling someone close to you, it is helpful to dial the number directly without using the speed button, or to write your diary on paper.
g_text_translate.de:
Digitale Demenz bezieht sich auf ein Phänomen, bei dem Erinnerungen plötzlich nicht mehr in den Sinn kommen, während man sich auf digitale Geräte verlässt. Wenn Sie beispielsweise ohne Taschenrechner nicht rechnen können oder wenn Sie den Liedtext nicht auf dem Karaoke-Bildschirm sehen, können Sie nicht singen. Wenn sich diese Situation immer wieder wiederholt, ist das nicht auf die leichte Schulter zu nehmen, denn es wird nicht nur schwer zu leben sein, auch die Lern- oder Arbeitsfähigkeit kann allmählich nachlassen. Um einer digitalen Demenz vorzubeugen, ist es besser, etwas zu tun, das das Gehirn so weit wie möglich direkt stimuliert. Wenn Sie jemanden in Ihrer Nähe anrufen, ist es hilfreich, die Nummer direkt zu wählen, ohne die Kurzwahltaste zu verwenden, oder Ihr Tagebuch auf Papier zu schreiben.

数字痴呆是指在依赖数字设备时突然无法想起记忆的现象。例如，如果没有计算器无法计算，或者在卡拉 OK 屏幕上看不到歌词，则无法唱歌。如果这种情况一而再再而三的发生，也不是什么可以掉以轻心的事情，因为不仅生活困难，学习或工作能力也可能会逐渐下降。为了预防数字痴呆，最好尽可能做一些直接刺激大脑的事情。呼叫亲近的人时，不使用快速键直接拨打号码或将日记写在纸上会很有帮助。

A demência digital refere-se a um fenômeno no qual as memórias repentinamente não vêm à mente enquanto você depende de dispositivos digitais. Por exemplo, se você não consegue calcular sem uma calculadora ou se não vê a letra na tela do karaokê, você não pode cantar. g_text_translate.hi:
डिजिटल डिमेंशिया एक ऐसी घटना को संदर्भित करता है जिसमें डिजिटल उपकरणों पर निर्भर रहने पर यादें अचानक दिमाग में नहीं आती हैं। उदाहरण के लिए, यदि आप कैलकुलेटर के बिना गणना नहीं कर सकते हैं, या यदि आप कराओके स्क्रीन पर गीत के बोल नहीं देखते हैं, तो आप गा नहीं सकते हैं। यदि यह स्थिति बार-बार दोहराई जाती है, तो इसे हल्के में लेने की बात नहीं है, क्योंकि न केवल जीना मुश्किल हो जाएगा, बल्कि सीखने या काम करने की क्षमता भी धीरे-धीरे कम हो सकती है। डिजिटल डिमेंशिया को रोकने के लिए, कुछ ऐसा करना बेहतर है जो सीधे मस्तिष्क को यथासंभव उत्तेजित करे। अपने किसी करीबी को कॉल करते समय, स्पीड बटन का उपयोग किए बिना सीधे नंबर डायल करना, या कागज पर अपनी डायरी लिखना सहायक होता है।

Dijital demans, dijital cihazlara güvenirken anıların aniden akla gelmemesi olgusunu ifade eder. Örneğin hesap makinesi olmadan hesap yapamıyorsanız veya karaoke ekranında şarkı sözlerini göremiyorsanız şarkı söyleyemezsiniz. Bu durumun defalarca tekrarlanması hafife alınacak bir şey değildir, çünkü hem yaşamak zorlaşacak hem de öğrenme veya çalışma yeteneği giderek azalabilecektir. Dijital demansı önlemek için mümkün olduğunca beyni doğrudan uyaran bir şeyler yapmak daha iyidir. Yakınınızdaki birini ararken, hız düğmesini kullanmadan numarayı doğrudan çevirmeniz veya günlüğünüzü bir kağıda yazmanız yararlı olacaktır.

डिजिटल डिमेंशिया डिजिटल उपकरणों पर निर्भरता के कारण होता है, इसलिए इसे रोकने के लिए मस्तिष्क की गतिविधि को उत्तेजित करना आवश्यक है। निम्नलिखित वाक्य एक उदाहरण भी देता है कि फ़ोन नंबर डायल करना या जर्नलिंग करना भी मस्तिष्क की गतिविधि है।

#######

Choose the correct one to fill in ( ).
1. That you have to do repeatedly
2. That is directly related to work
3. That you can do with other people
4. That directly stimulates the brain
--------------------
<g>뇌:</g> Brain
<g>자극하다:</g> To stimulate

Digital dementia occurs due to dependence on digital devices, so to prevent it, it is necessary to stimulate brain activity. The following sentence also gives an example that dialing a phone number or journaling is also brain activity.
explain.tr:
Doldurmak için doğru olanı seçin ( ).
1. Tekrar tekrar yapmanız gereken şey
2. Bu doğrudan işle ilgilidir
3. Diğer insanlarla yapabileceğiniz şeyler
4. Beyni doğrudan uyarır
--------------------
<g>뇌:</g>Beyin
<g>자극하다:</g>Teşvik etmek

Dijital demans, dijital cihazlara bağımlılık nedeniyle ortaya çıkar, bu nedenle bunu önlemek için beyin aktivitesini uyarmak gerekir. Aşağıdaki cümle aynı zamanda bir telefon numarasını çevirmenin veya günlük tutmanın da beyin aktivitesi olduğuna dair bir örnek vermektedir.

#######

Choose the correct one to fill in ( ).
1. That you have to do repeatedly
2. That is directly related to work
3. That you can do with other people
4. That directly stimulates the brain
--------------------
<g>뇌:</g> Brain
<g>자극하다:</g> To stimulate

Digital dementia occurs due to dependence on digital devices, so to prevent it, it is necessary to stimulate brain activity. The following sentence also gives an example that dialing a phone number or journaling is also brain activity.

general.g_text: 스포츠 관람 인구가 늘어남에 따라 스포츠는 기업의 중요한 광고 수단이 되고 있다. 기업의 입장에서 스포츠 마케팅은 선수의 운동복에 기업 이름을 넣거나 경기장에 광고문을 설치하여 사람들이 경기를 보는 동안 계속해서 광고를 보게 할 수 있는 장점이 있다. 그리고 스포츠 정신을 기업의 경영 원리와 연결시켜 이미지를 홍보할 수도 있고, 선수를 광고 모델로 삼아 제품의 판매율을 높일 수 있다. 한편 운동선수도 스포츠 마케팅을 통해 얻을 수 있는 것이 많다. 선수 개인으로서는 수입이 생겨 운동에 더 전념할 수 있다. 또한 광고 모델로 인기를 끌면 사람들의 관심을 더 많이 받을 수 있어서 좋다.

g_text_translate.vi: Số người dân xem thể thao ngày càng tăng nên thể thao đang trở thành phương tiện quảng cáo quan trọng của doanh nghiệp. Trên lập trường của doanh nghiệp, marketing qua thể thao có điểm mạnh là khi để tên doanh nghiệp lên đồ của vận động viên hay cài đặt câu quảng cáo ở sân thi đấu thì người ta có thể thấy quảng cáo trong suốt lúc xem thi đấu. Và có thể liên kết tinh thần thể thao với nguyên lý kinh doanh của doanh nghiệp và quảng bá hình ảnh, cũng có thể lấy vận động viên làm người mẫu quảng cáo và gia tăng tỷ  lệ bán ra của sản phẩm. Mặt khác, vận động viên cũng có thể nhận được nhiều thứ thông qua marketing thể thao. Vận động viên có khoản thu nhập cá nhân nên có thể chuyên tâm hơn vào thể thao. Thêm vào đó, họ có thể thu hút sự chú ý với vai trò người mẫu và nhận được nhiều sự quan tâm hơn nên cũng rất tốt.
g_text_translate.en: As the sports watching population increases, sports are becoming an important advertising medium for companies. From the company's point of view, sports marketing has the advantage of putting the company name on the athletes' sportswear or installing advertisements in the stadium so that people can continue to see the advertisement while watching the game. In addition, by linking the spirit of sports with the management principle of the company, the image can be promoted, and the sales rate of the product can be increased by using the athlete as an advertising model. Athletes, on the other hand, have a lot to gain through sports marketing. As an individual athlete, you can earn money and focus more on sports. It's also good that you can get more people's attention if you become popular as an advertising model.
g_text_translate.fr:
À mesure que la population de spectateurs sportifs augmente, le sport devient un support publicitaire important pour les entreprises. Du point de vue de l'entreprise, le marketing sportif a l'avantage de mettre le nom de l'entreprise sur les vêtements de sport des athlètes ou d'installer des publicités dans le stade afin que les gens puissent continuer à voir la publicité tout en regardant le match. De plus, en liant l'esprit sportif au principe de gestion de l'entreprise, l'image peut être promue et le taux de vente du produit peut être augmenté en utilisant l'athlète comme modèle publicitaire. Les athlètes, en revanche, ont beaucoup à gagner grâce au marketing sportif. En tant qu'athlète individuel, vous pouvez gagner de l'argent et vous concentrer davantage sur le sport. Il est également bon que vous puissiez attirer davantage l'attention des gens si vous devenez populaire en tant que modèle publicitaire.

#######

As the sports watching population increases, sports are becoming an important advertising medium for companies. From the company's point of view, sports marketing has the advantage of putting the company name on the athletes' sportswear or installing advertisements in the stadium so that people can continue to see the advertisement while watching the game. In addition, by linking the spirit of sports with the management principle of the company, the image can be promoted, and the sales rate of the product can be increased by using the athlete as an advertising model. Athletes, on the other hand, have a lot to gain through sports marketing. As an individual athlete, you can earn money and focus more on sports. It's also good that you can get more people's attention if you become popular as an advertising model.
g_text_translate.es:
A medida que aumenta la población que mira deportes, los deportes se están convirtiendo en un medio publicitario importante para las empresas. Desde el punto de vista de la empresa, el marketing deportivo tiene la ventaja de poner el nombre de la empresa en la ropa deportiva de los atletas o instalar anuncios en el estadio para que la gente pueda seguir viendo el anuncio mientras ve el partido. Además, al vincular el espíritu deportivo con el principio de gestión de la empresa, se puede promover la imagen y aumentar la tasa de ventas del producto utilizando al atleta como modelo publicitario. Los atletas, por otro lado, tienen mucho que ganar a través del marketing deportivo. Como atleta individual, puedes ganar dinero y concentrarte más en los deportes. También es bueno que puedas llamar la atención de más personas si te vuelves popular como modelo publicitario.

g_text_translate.id:
Dengan meningkatnya populasi menonton olahraga, olahraga menjadi media periklanan penting bagi perusahaan. Dari sudut pandang perusahaan, pemasaran olahraga memiliki keuntungan dengan menempatkan nama perusahaan pada pakaian olahraga atlet atau memasang iklan di stadion sehingga orang dapat terus melihat iklan tersebut sambil menonton pertandingan. Selain itu, dengan mengaitkan semangat olahraga dengan prinsip manajemen perusahaan, citra dapat dipromosikan, dan tingkat penjualan produk dapat ditingkatkan dengan menggunakan atlet sebagai model iklan. Atlet, di sisi lain, memiliki banyak keuntungan melalui pemasaran olahraga. Sebagai atlet individu, Anda bisa mendapatkan uang dan lebih fokus pada olahraga. Ada baiknya juga Anda bisa mendapatkan lebih banyak perhatian orang jika Anda menjadi populer sebagai model periklanan.

g_text_translate.ja:
スポーツ観戦人口の増加に伴い、スポーツは企業にとって重要な広告媒体となっています。企業側からすれば、スポーツマーケティングには、選手のスポーツウェアに社名を入れたり、スタジアムに広告を設置したりして、人々が試合を見ながら広告を見続けることができるという利点があります。また、スポーツ精神を企業の経営理念に結びつけることで、イメージアップを図ることができ、アスリートを広告モデルとして起用することで商品の販売率を高めることができます。一方、アスリートは、スポーツ マーケティングを通じて多くのことを得ることができます。個々のアスリートとして、お金を稼ぎ、スポーツにより集中することができます。広告モデルとして人気になれば、もっと多くの人に注目してもらえるのもいいですね。

g_text_translate.de:
Da die Zahl der Sportbeobachter zunimmt, wird Sport zu einem wichtigen Werbemedium für Unternehmen. Sportmarketing hat aus Sicht des Unternehmens den Vorteil, den Firmennamen auf der Sportbekleidung der Athleten anzubringen oder Werbung im Stadion zu installieren, damit die Menschen die Werbung während des Spiels weiterhin sehen können. Zudem kann durch die Verknüpfung des Sportsgeistes mit dem Führungsprinzip des Unternehmens das Image gefördert und die Abverkaufsrate des Produkts durch den Einsatz des Sportlers als Werbeträger gesteigert werden. Sportler hingegen haben durch Sportmarketing viel zu gewinnen. Als Einzelsportler kannst du Geld verdienen und dich mehr auf den Sport konzentrieren. Gut ist auch, dass man mehr Aufmerksamkeit erregen kann, wenn man als Werbemodel beliebt wird.

随着体育观众人数的增加，体育正在成为企业重要的广告媒介。从公司的角度来看，体育营销的优势在于将公司名称放在运动员的运动服上，或者在体育场内安装广告，让人们在观看比赛的同时还能继续看到广告。此外，通过将体育精神与公司的经营理念相结合，可以提升形象，以运动员为广告模式，提高产品的销售率。另一方面，运动员可以通过体育营销获得很多收益。作为个人运动员，您可以赚钱并更加专注于运动。作为广告模特走红，能引起更多人的关注也是好事。

g_text_translate.ru:
По мере того, как население, наблюдающее за спортом, увеличивается, спорт становится важным рекламным средством для компаний. С точки зрения компании, спортивный маркетинг имеет преимущество, заключающееся в размещении названия компании на спортивной одежде спортсменов или установке рекламных объявлений на стадионе, чтобы люди могли продолжать видеть рекламу во время просмотра игры. Кроме того, связав дух спорта с принципом управления компанией, можно повысить имидж и повысить уровень продаж продукта, используя спортсмена в качестве рекламной модели. С другой стороны, спортсмены могут многое получить от спортивного маркетинга. Как индивидуальный спортсмен, вы можете зарабатывать деньги и больше сосредоточиться на спорте. Хорошо также, что вы сможете привлечь больше внимания людей, если станете популярными как рекламная модель.

เนื่องจากจำนวนผู้ชมกีฬาเพิ่มมากขึ้น กีฬาจึงกลายเป็นสื่อโฆษณาที่สำคัญสำหรับบริษัทต่างๆ ในมุมมองของบริษัท การตลาดด้านกีฬามีข้อได้เปรียบในการใส่ชื่อบริษัทบนชุดกีฬาของนักกีฬา หรือติดโฆษณาในสนามกีฬาเพื่อให้ผู้คนสามารถเห็นโฆษณาต่อไปในขณะที่ดูการแข่งขันได้ นอกจากนี้การเชื่อมโยงจิตวิญญาณของการกีฬาเข้ากับหลักการบริหารจัดการของบริษัทสามารถส่งเสริมภาพลักษณ์และเพิ่มยอดขายของผลิตภัณฑ์ได้โดยใช้นักกีฬาเป็นแบบอย่างในการโฆษณา ในทางกลับกัน นักกีฬาจะได้รับประโยชน์มากมายจากการตลาดด้านกีฬา ในฐานะนักกีฬารายบุคคล คุณสามารถสร้างรายได้และมุ่งความสนใจไปที่กีฬาได้มากขึ้น นอกจากนี้ยังเป็นเรื่องดีที่คุณสามารถได้รับความสนใจจากผู้คนมากขึ้นหากคุณได้รับความนิยมในฐานะรูปแบบการโฆษณา

g_text_translate.hi:
जैसे-जैसे खेल देखने वाली आबादी बढ़ रही है, खेल कंपनियों के लिए एक महत्वपूर्ण विज्ञापन माध्यम बनता जा रहा है। कंपनी के दृष्टिकोण से, स्पोर्ट्स मार्केटिंग में एथलीटों के स्पोर्ट्सवियर पर कंपनी का नाम लगाने या स्टेडियम में विज्ञापन लगाने का फायदा है ताकि लोग खेल देखते समय विज्ञापन देखना जारी रख सकें। इसके अलावा, खेल की भावना को कंपनी के प्रबंधन सिद्धांत के साथ जोड़कर छवि को बढ़ावा दिया जा सकता है, और एथलीट को विज्ञापन मॉडल के रूप में उपयोग करके उत्पाद की बिक्री दर को बढ़ाया जा सकता है। दूसरी ओर, एथलीटों को खेल विपणन के माध्यम से बहुत कुछ हासिल होता है। एक व्यक्तिगत एथलीट के रूप में, आप पैसा कमा सकते हैं और खेल पर अधिक ध्यान केंद्रित कर सकते हैं। यह भी अच्छा है कि यदि आप एक विज्ञापन मॉडल के रूप में लोकप्रिय हो जाते हैं तो आप अधिक लोगों का ध्यान आकर्षित कर सकते हैं।

#######

As the sports watching population increases, sports are becoming an important advertising medium for companies. From the company's point of view, sports marketing has the advantage of putting the company name on the athletes' sportswear or installing advertisements in the stadium so that people can continue to see the advertisement while watching the game. In addition, by linking the spirit of sports with the management principle of the company, the image can be promoted, and the sales rate of the product can be increased by using the athlete as an advertising model. Athletes, on the other hand, have a lot to gain through sports marketing. As an individual athlete, you can earn money and focus more on sports. It's also good that you can get more people's attention if you become popular as an advertising model.
g_text_translate.tr:
Spor izleyen nüfusun artmasıyla birlikte spor, şirketler için önemli bir reklam mecrası haline geliyor. Şirket açısından bakıldığında, spor pazarlaması, şirket adının sporcuların spor kıyafetlerine konulması veya insanların maçı izlerken reklamı görmeye devam edebilmeleri için stadyuma reklam yerleştirme avantajına sahiptir. Ayrıca spor ruhunun şirketin yönetim prensibiyle ilişkilendirilmesiyle sporcunun reklam modeli olarak kullanılmasıyla imaj yükseltilebilir, ürünün satış oranı artırılabilir. Öte yandan sporcuların spor pazarlaması yoluyla kazanacakları çok şey var. Bireysel sporcu olarak para kazanabilir ve spora daha çok odaklanabilirsiniz. Bir reklam modeli olarak popüler olursanız daha fazla insanın dikkatini çekebilmeniz de iyidir.

#######

As the sports watching population increases, sports are becoming an important advertising medium for companies. From the company's point of view, sports marketing has the advantage of putting the company name on the athletes' sportswear or installing advertisements in the stadium so that people can continue to see the advertisement while watching the game. In addition, by linking the spirit of sports with the management principle of the company, the image can be promoted, and the sales rate of the product can be increased by using the athlete as an advertising model. Athletes, on the other hand, have a lot to gain through sports marketing. As an individual athlete, you can earn money and focus more on sports. It's also good that you can get more people's attention if you become popular as an advertising model.

content.q_text: 이 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 스포츠 마케팅 덕분에 스포츠 관람 인구가 증가하고 있다.
기업들은 스포츠 정신을 알리기 위해 홍보 활동에 힘쓰고 있다.
경기장에 광고문을 설치하면 사람들이 자연스럽게 광고를 접하게 된다.
운동선수를 출연시킨 광고는 해당 종목의 이미지를 높이는 데 효과적이다.

explain.vi:
Chọn câu giống với nội dung trên.
1. Nhờ có marketing thể thao mà số người xem thể thao đang tăng lên.
2. Các doanh nghiệp đang dồn sức vào hoạt động quảng bá để tuyên truyền tinh thần thể thao.
3. Nếu cài đặt câu quảng cáo ở sân thi đấu thì người ta có thể tiếp cận quảng cáo một cách tự nhiên.
4. Quảng cáo có vận động viên xuất hiện tạo hiệu quả nâng cao hình ảnh của môn thể thao đó.
--------------------
자연스럽게: Một cách tự nhiên
계속하다: Liên tục
V-는 동안: Trong lúc...

경기장에 광고문을 설치하여 사람들이 경기를 보는 동안 계속해서 광고를 보게 할 수 있는 장점이 있다: Cài đặt câu quảng cáo ở sân thi đấu thì người ta có thể thấy quảng cáo trong suốt lúc xem thi đấu.
explain.en:

2. Businesses are focusing on promotional activities to propagate the sports spirit.
3. If you install the ad in the field, people can reach the ad naturally.
4. Ads with athletes appearing effectively enhance the image of that sport.

1. Spor pazarlaması sayesinde spor izleyenlerin sayısı artıyor.
2. İşletmeler spor ruhunu yaymak için tanıtım faaliyetlerine ağırlık veriyor.
3. Reklamı alana yerleştirirseniz insanlar reklama doğal bir şekilde ulaşabilir.
4. Sporcuların yer aldığı reklamlar o sporun imajını etkili bir şekilde geliştirir.
--------------------
<g>자연스럽게:</g>doğal olarak
<g>계속하다:</g>Devam etmek

<g>V + 는 동안:</g>Sırasında...

<g>경기장에 광고문을 설치하여 사람들이 경기를 보는 동안 계속해서 광고를 보게 할 수 있는 장점이 있다:</g>İnsanların maç izlerken reklamı görmeye devam edebilmeleri için stadyuma reklamlar yerleştirilebilir.

#######

Choose one that matches the content of this paragraph.
1. Thanks to sports marketing, the number of people watching sports is increasing.
2. Businesses are focusing on promotional activities to propagate the sports spirit.
3. If you install the ad in the field, people can reach the ad naturally.
4. Ads with athletes appearing effectively enhance the image of that sport.
--------------------
<g>자연스럽게:</g> Naturally
<g>계속하다:</g> To continue

<g>V + 는 동안:</g> While...

<g>경기장에 광고문을 설치하여 사람들이 경기를 보는 동안 계속해서 광고를 보게 할 수 있는 장점이 있다:</g> Advertisements can be installed in the stadium so that people can continue to see the ad while watching the game.

general.g_text: 앞서 살펴본 것처럼 현재 ‘방과 후 학교’에서 운영되고 있는 교육 프로그램은 학생들도 많이 참여하고 학부모의 호응도 좋기 때문에 방과 후 학교를 확대하여 실시할 필요가 있다. 특히 방과 후 학교는 보충 수업이나 학교 수업의 연장이 아니라 인성교육의 장으로 활용될 수 있다는 점을 강조하고 싶다. 방과 후 학교는 정규 수업 시간과 달리 분위기가 자유롭기 때문에 교사와 학생이 평소에는 잘 나눌 수 없었던 이야기를 주고받게 된다. 또한 방과 후 학교에서는 또래 친구들과 자연스럽게 어울릴 수 있는 시간을 가질 수 있으므로 학생들에게 놀이 프로그램을 통해 협동심과 사회성을 길러 줄 수도 있다.

g_text_translate.vi: Như đã phân tích ở phần trước, chương trình giáo dục đang được thực hiện ở "Trường học sau giờ học" hiện tại đang có nhiều học sinh tham gia và sự hưởng ứng của phụ huynh cũng rất tốt, vì vậy cần mở rộng thực hiện trường học sau giờ học. Đặc biệt trường học sau giờ học nhấn mạnh rằng họ có thể sử dụng làm chương trình giáo dục nhân cách chứ không phải là lớp học thêm hay kéo dài thời gian học. Ở trường học sau giờ học, bầu không khí sẽ tự do, khác với thời gian học chính quy, vì vậy giáo viên và học sinh có thể trao đổi những câu chuyện mà bình thường họ không chia sẻ được. Thêm vào đó, ở trường học sau giờ học, học sinh có thể có thời gian hòa nhập một cách tự nhiên với bạn đồng trang lứa nên có thể nuôi dưỡng tinh thần hợp tác và kỹ năng mềm cho học sinh thông qua chương trình trò chơi.
g_text_translate.en: As we have seen above, the educational programs currently being operated in ‘After-school schools’ need to be expanded and implemented because many students participate and parents are well-received. In particular, I want to emphasize that the after-school school can be used as a place for character education rather than supplementary classes or extension of school classes. After school, the school has a free atmosphere, unlike regular class hours, so teachers and students exchange stories that they would not normally be able to share. In addition, after school, students can have time to socialize naturally with their peers, so it is possible to develop cooperative spirit and social skills through play programs in students.
g_text_translate.fr:
Comme nous l'avons vu ci-dessus, les programmes éducatifs actuellement mis en œuvre dans les «écoles périscolaires» doivent être étendus et mis en œuvre car de nombreux élèves participent et les parents sont bien accueillis. En particulier, je tiens à souligner que l'école périscolaire peut être utilisée comme un lieu d'éducation du caractère plutôt que comme des cours supplémentaires ou une extension des classes scolaires. Après l'école, l'école a une atmosphère libre, contrairement aux heures de cours régulières, de sorte que les enseignants et les élèves échangent des histoires qu'ils ne pourraient normalement pas partager. De plus, après l'école, les élèves peuvent avoir le temps de socialiser naturellement avec leurs pairs, il est donc possible de développer l'esprit coopératif et les compétences sociales grâce à des programmes de jeu chez les élèves.

#######

As we have seen above, the educational programs currently being operated in ‘After-school schools’ need to be expanded and implemented because many students participate and parents are well-received. In particular, I want to emphasize that the after-school school can be used as a place for character education rather than supplementary classes or extension of school classes. After school, the school has a free atmosphere, unlike regular class hours, so teachers and students exchange stories that they would not normally be able to share. In addition, after school, students can have time to socialize naturally with their peers, so it is possible to develop cooperative spirit and social skills through play programs in students.
g_text_translate.es:
Como hemos visto anteriormente, los programas educativos que se llevan a cabo actualmente en las “Escuelas extraescolares” deben expandirse e implementarse porque muchos estudiantes participan y los padres son bien recibidos. En particular, quiero enfatizar que la escuela extraescolar puede usarse como un lugar para la educación del carácter en lugar de clases complementarias o extensión de las clases escolares. Después de la escuela, la escuela tiene un ambiente libre, a diferencia de las horas regulares de clase, por lo que los maestros y los estudiantes intercambian historias que normalmente no podrían compartir. Además, después de la escuela, los estudiantes pueden tener tiempo para socializar de forma natural con sus compañeros, por lo que es posible desarrollar el espíritu cooperativo y las habilidades sociales a través de programas de juego en los estudiantes.

g_text_translate.id:
Seperti yang telah kita lihat di atas, program pendidikan yang saat ini dijalankan di 'Sekolah setelah sekolah' perlu diperluas dan dilaksanakan karena banyak siswa yang berpartisipasi dan orang tua diterima dengan baik. Secara khusus, saya ingin tekankan bahwa sekolah sepulang sekolah dapat digunakan sebagai tempat pendidikan karakter daripada kelas tambahan atau perpanjangan kelas sekolah. Sepulang sekolah, suasana sekolah bebas, tidak seperti jam pelajaran biasa, sehingga guru dan siswa saling bertukar cerita yang biasanya tidak bisa mereka bagikan. Selain itu, sepulang sekolah, siswa dapat memiliki waktu untuk bersosialisasi secara alami dengan teman sebayanya, sehingga dimungkinkan untuk mengembangkan jiwa kooperatif dan keterampilan sosial melalui program bermain pada siswa.

g_text_translate.ja:
これまで見てきたように、現在「放課後学校」で実施されている教育プログラムは、多くの生徒が参加し、保護者に好評であるため、拡大して実施する必要があります。特に放課後学校は補習や授業の延長ではなく、人格教育の場として利用できることを強調したい。放課後は通常の授業時間とは異なり、自由な雰囲気の学校で、先生と生徒が普段は話せないような話を交わします。また、放課後は仲間と自然に交流する時間を持つことができるため、遊びを通じて協調性や社会性を育むことができます。

g_text_translate.de:
Wie wir oben gesehen haben, müssen die Bildungsprogramme, die derzeit in „Nachschulischen Schulen“ betrieben werden, erweitert und umgesetzt werden, da viele Schüler teilnehmen und Eltern gut angenommen werden. Besonders möchte ich betonen, dass der Hort als Ort der Charakterbildung und nicht als Ergänzungsunterricht oder Erweiterung der Schulklassen genutzt werden kann. Nach der Schule hat die Schule im Gegensatz zu den regulären Unterrichtsstunden eine freie Atmosphäre, sodass Lehrer und Schüler Geschichten austauschen, die sie normalerweise nicht teilen könnten. Darüber hinaus können die Schüler nach der Schule Zeit haben, sich auf natürliche Weise mit Gleichaltrigen zu treffen, sodass es möglich ist, kooperativen Geist und soziale Fähigkeiten durch Spielprogramme bei den Schülern zu entwickeln.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
正如我们在上面看到的，目前在“课外学校”开展的教育项目需要扩大和实施，因为有很多学生参与，家长也很受欢迎。我特别要强调的是，课后学校可以作为品格教育的场所，而不是补课或学校课的延伸。放学后，学校有一个自由的氛围，不像正常的上课时间，所以老师和学生会交换他们通常无法分享的故事。此外，放学后，学生可以有时间自然地与同龄人交往，因此可以通过学生的游戏项目来培养合作精神和社交技能。

g_text_translate.ru:
Как мы видели выше, образовательные программы, реализуемые в настоящее время в «дошкольных учреждениях», необходимо расширять и реализовывать, поскольку в них участвует много учащихся и их хорошо принимают родители. В частности, хочу подчеркнуть, что продленная школа может использоваться как место воспитания характера, а не как дополнительные занятия или продление школьных занятий. После уроков в школе царит свободная атмосфера, в отличие от обычных занятий, поэтому учителя и ученики обмениваются историями, которыми обычно они не смогли бы поделиться. Кроме того, после школы у учащихся может быть время для естественного общения со своими сверстниками, поэтому можно развивать у учащихся дух сотрудничества и социальные навыки с помощью игровых программ.

ดังที่เราได้เห็นข้างต้น โครงการการศึกษาที่กำลังดำเนินการใน "โรงเรียนหลังเลิกเรียน" ในปัจจุบันจำเป็นต้องได้รับการขยายและดำเนินการ เนื่องจากนักเรียนจำนวนมากเข้าร่วมและผู้ปกครองก็ได้รับการตอบรับอย่างดี โดยเฉพาะอย่างยิ่ง ฉันต้องการเน้นย้ำว่าโรงเรียนหลังเลิกเรียนสามารถใช้เป็นสถานที่สำหรับการศึกษาลักษณะนิสัย แทนที่จะเป็นชั้นเรียนเสริมหรือขยายชั้นเรียนในโรงเรียน หลังเลิกเรียน โรงเรียนมีบรรยากาศที่เป็นอิสระ ไม่เหมือนเวลาเรียนปกติ ครูและนักเรียนจึงได้แลกเปลี่ยนเรื่องราวที่ปกติแล้วพวกเขาจะไม่สามารถแบ่งปันได้ นอกจากนี้ หลังเลิกเรียน นักเรียนยังสามารถมีเวลาพบปะสังสรรค์กับเพื่อนฝูงอย่างเป็นธรรมชาติ ดังนั้นจึงเป็นไปได้ที่จะพัฒนาจิตวิญญาณแห่งความร่วมมือและทักษะทางสังคมผ่านโปรแกรมการเล่นในนักเรียน

Como vimos acima, os programas educativos actualmente em funcionamento nas “escolas pós-escolares” precisam de ser expandidos e implementados porque muitos estudantes participam e os pais são bem recebidos. Em particular, quero enfatizar que a escola extracurricular pode ser usada como um local para a educação do caráter, em vez de aulas complementares ou extensão das aulas escolares. Depois das aulas, a escola tem um ambiente livre, diferentemente do horário normal de aula, por isso professores e alunos trocam histórias que normalmente não conseguiriam partilhar. Além disso, depois das aulas, os alunos podem ter tempo para socializar naturalmente com os seus pares, sendo assim possível desenvolver o espírito cooperativo e as competências sociais através de programas lúdicos nos alunos.

g_text_translate.hi:
जैसा कि हमने ऊपर देखा है, वर्तमान में 'स्कूल के बाद के स्कूलों' में संचालित किए जा रहे शैक्षिक कार्यक्रमों को विस्तारित और कार्यान्वित करने की आवश्यकता है क्योंकि कई छात्र भाग लेते हैं और माता-पिता का अच्छा स्वागत होता है। विशेष रूप से, मैं इस बात पर जोर देना चाहता हूं कि स्कूल के बाद के स्कूल का उपयोग पूरक कक्षाओं या स्कूल कक्षाओं के विस्तार के बजाय चरित्र शिक्षा के लिए एक स्थान के रूप में किया जा सकता है। स्कूल के बाद, नियमित कक्षा घंटों के विपरीत, स्कूल में एक स्वतंत्र माहौल होता है, इसलिए शिक्षक और छात्र उन कहानियों का आदान-प्रदान करते हैं जिन्हें वे आम तौर पर साझा नहीं कर पाएंगे। इसके अलावा, स्कूल के बाद, छात्रों को अपने साथियों के साथ स्वाभाविक रूप से मेलजोल करने का समय मिल सकता है, इसलिए छात्रों में खेल कार्यक्रमों के माध्यम से सहकारी भावना और सामाजिक कौशल विकसित करना संभव है।

g_text_translate.tr:
Yukarıda da gördüğümüz gibi, hâlihazırda 'Okul sonrası okullarda' yürütülen eğitim programlarının, çok sayıda öğrencinin katılımı ve velilerin olumlu karşılanması nedeniyle genişletilmesi ve uygulanması gerekiyor. Özellikle okul sonrası okulun ek ders veya okul derslerinin genişletilmesinden ziyade karakter eğitimi için bir yer olarak kullanılabileceğini vurgulamak istiyorum. Okuldan sonra, normal ders saatlerinin aksine okulda özgür bir atmosfer vardır, bu nedenle öğretmenler ve öğrenciler normalde paylaşamayacakları hikayeleri paylaşırlar. Ayrıca öğrenciler okuldan sonra akranlarıyla doğal bir şekilde sosyalleşmeye zaman ayırabilir, böylece öğrencilerde oyun programları aracılığıyla işbirlikçi ruhun ve sosyal becerilerin geliştirilmesi mümkün olur.

#######

As we have seen above, the educational programs currently being operated in ‘After-school schools’ need to be expanded and implemented because many students participate and parents are well-received. In particular, I want to emphasize that the after-school school can be used as a place for character education rather than supplementary classes or extension of school classes. After school, the school has a free atmosphere, unlike regular class hours, so teachers and students exchange stories that they would not normally be able to share. In addition, after school, students can have time to socialize naturally with their peers, so it is possible to develop cooperative spirit and social skills through play programs in students.

q_answer: 방과 후 학교의 교육 프로그램을 다양하게 개발해야 한다.
혼자 있는 아이들을 위해 방과 후 학교의 운영 시간을 늘려야 한다.
방과 후 학교의 프로그램을 확대하면 학생 참여율을 더 높일 수 있다.
방과 후 학교는 학생들의 인성교육을 강화하는 데 큰 역할을 할 수 있다.

general.g_text: 문학 교육의 의의는 작품을 통해 타인을 이해하고 더불어 사는 지혜를 가르침으로써 삶의 진정한 의미를 깨닫게 해 주는 데 있다. 작품을 읽고 재미와 감동을 느낌으로써 학생들은 삶의 아름다움을 느끼며 희망과 용기도 얻는다. 그런데 요즘 중.고교의 문학 교육은 학생들로 하여금 문학을 어렵기만하고 재미는 없는 것으로 인식하게 하고 있다. 작품에 밑줄을 치고 무조건 외우게 함으로써 학생들이 문학에 흥미를 느낄 수 없게 만들고 있는 것이다. 학생들에게 작품을 읽고 생각할 시간이, 그리고 자신의 생각을 표현할 기회가 충분히 주어져야 한다. 그때에 비로소 학생들은 (        ) 수 있을 것이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Ý nghĩa của giáo dục văn học là giúp học sinh hiểu về người khác và nhận ra ý nghĩa thật sự của cuộc sống bằng cách dạy về sự khôn ngoan trong đời thông qua tác phẩm. Khi đọc tác phẩm và cảm nhận được sự thú vị và cảm động, học sinh sẽ nhận ra vẻ đẹp của cuộc sống, có được dũng khí và hy vọng. Thế nhưng gần đây việc giáo dục văn học ở các trường cấp 2 và cấp 3 lại khiến học sinh thấy rằng văn học chỉ khó và nhàm chán. Việc bắt học sinh gạch chân dưới tác phẩm rồi bắt học thuộc lòng vô điều kiện khiến học sinh không cảm thấy hứng thú với tác phẩm. Cần phải cho học sinh thời gian để đọc và nghĩ về tác phẩm cũng như cơ hội thể hiện suy nghĩ của bản thân. Đến lúc đó thì học sinh mới có thể  (        ).
g_text_translate.en: The significance of literature education is to help us understand the true meaning of life by teaching the wisdom of understanding others and living together through works. By reading the works and feeling the fun and emotion, students feel the beauty of life and gain hope and courage. However, these days, literature education in middle and high schools is making students perceive literature as difficult and not interesting. By underlining the works and making them memorize them unconditionally, they are making the students less interested in literature. Students should be given ample time to read and think about the work and to express their thoughts. Only then will students be able to fully enjoy literature.
g_text_translate.fr:
L'importance de l'enseignement de la littérature est de nous aider à comprendre le vrai sens de la vie en enseignant la sagesse de comprendre les autres et de vivre ensemble à travers les œuvres. En lisant les œuvres et en ressentant le plaisir et l'émotion, les élèves ressentent la beauté de la vie et gagnent espoir et courage. Cependant, de nos jours, l'enseignement de la littérature dans les collèges et lycées fait que les élèves perçoivent la littérature comme difficile et pas intéressante. En soulignant les œuvres et en les faisant mémoriser inconditionnellement, ils désintéressent les élèves pour la littérature. Les élèves doivent disposer de suffisamment de temps pour lire et réfléchir au travail et pour exprimer leurs pensées. Ce n'est qu'alors que les élèves pourront profiter pleinement de la littérature.

#######

The significance of literature education is to help us understand the true meaning of life by teaching the wisdom of understanding others and living together through works. By reading the works and feeling the fun and emotion, students feel the beauty of life and gain hope and courage. However, these days, literature education in middle and high schools is making students perceive literature as difficult and not interesting. By underlining the works and making them memorize them unconditionally, they are making the students less interested in literature. Students should be given ample time to read and think about the work and to express their thoughts. Only then will students be able to fully enjoy literature.
g_text_translate.es:
La importancia de la educación literaria es ayudarnos a comprender el verdadero significado de la vida al enseñar la sabiduría de comprender a los demás y vivir juntos a través de las obras. Al leer las obras y sentir la diversión y la emoción, los estudiantes sienten la belleza de la vida y adquieren esperanza y coraje. Sin embargo, en estos días, la educación literaria en las escuelas intermedias y secundarias hace que los estudiantes perciban la literatura como algo difícil y poco interesante. Al subrayar las obras y hacer que las memoricen incondicionalmente, están haciendo que los estudiantes se interesen menos por la literatura. Los estudiantes deben tener suficiente tiempo para leer y pensar sobre el trabajo y expresar sus pensamientos. Solo así los alumnos podrán disfrutar plenamente de la literatura.

#######

The significance of literature education is to help us understand the true meaning of life by teaching the wisdom of understanding others and living together through works. By reading the works and feeling the fun and emotion, students feel the beauty of life and gain hope and courage. However, these days, literature education in middle and high schools is making students perceive literature as difficult and not interesting. By underlining the works and making them memorize them unconditionally, they are making the students less interested in literature. Students should be given ample time to read and think about the work and to express their thoughts. Only then will students be able to fully enjoy literature.
g_text_translate.id:
Arti penting pendidikan sastra adalah membantu kita memahami makna hidup yang sebenarnya dengan mengajarkan kearifan memahami orang lain dan hidup bersama melalui karya. Dengan membaca karya dan merasakan kesenangan dan emosi, siswa merasakan keindahan hidup dan memperoleh harapan dan keberanian. Namun, dewasa ini pendidikan sastra di SMP dan SMA membuat siswa menganggap sastra itu sulit dan tidak menarik. Dengan menggarisbawahi karya dan membuat mereka menghafalnya tanpa syarat, mereka membuat siswa kurang tertarik pada sastra. Siswa harus diberi waktu yang cukup untuk membaca dan berpikir tentang pekerjaan dan untuk mengekspresikan pikiran mereka. Hanya dengan demikian siswa dapat menikmati sastra sepenuhnya.

#######

The significance of literature education is to help us understand the true meaning of life by teaching the wisdom of understanding others and living together through works. By underlining the works and making them memorize them unconditionally, they are making the students less interested in literature. Students should be given ample time to read and think about the work and to express their thoughts. Only then will students be able to fully enjoy literature.
g_text_translate.ja:
文学教育の意義は、作品を通じて他者を理解し、共生する知恵を教えることによって、人生の真の意味を理解することにあります。作品を読んで楽しさや感動を覚えることで、生徒たちは生命の美しさを感じ、希望と勇気を得る。しかし、昨今、中学・高校での文学教育は、生徒たちに文学を難しく、面白くないものとして認識させています。作品に下線を引いて無条件に暗記させることで、学生の文学への関心を低下させている。生徒には、作品を読んで考え、自分の考えを表現するための十分な時間を与える必要があります。そうして初めて、生徒たちは文学を存分に楽しむことができるようになります。

#######

The significance of literature education is to help us understand the true meaning of life by teaching the wisdom of understanding others and living together through works. By reading the works and feeling the fun and emotion, students feel the beauty of life and gain hope and courage. However, these days, literature education in middle and high schools is making students perceive literature as difficult and not interesting. By underlining the works and making them memorize them unconditionally, they are making the students less interested in literature. Students should be given ample time to read and think about the work and to express their thoughts. Only then will students be able to fully enjoy literature.
g_text_translate.de:
Die Bedeutung der Literaturerziehung besteht darin, uns zu helfen, den wahren Sinn des Lebens zu verstehen, indem sie die Weisheit lehrt, andere zu verstehen und durch Werke zusammenzuleben. Indem sie die Werke lesen und den Spaß und die Emotionen spüren, spüren die Schüler die Schönheit des Lebens und gewinnen Hoffnung und Mut. Heutzutage führt der Literaturunterricht in Mittel- und Oberschulen jedoch dazu, dass Schüler Literatur als schwierig und nicht interessant wahrnehmen. Indem sie die Werke unterstreichen und sie bedingungslos auswendig lernen lassen, vermindern sie das Interesse der Schüler an Literatur. Den Schülern sollte ausreichend Zeit gegeben werden, die Arbeit zu lesen, darüber nachzudenken und ihre Gedanken auszudrücken. Nur dann können die Schüler die Literatur in vollem Umfang genießen.

#######

The significance of literature education is to help us understand the true meaning of life by teaching the wisdom of understanding others and living together through works. By reading the works and feeling the fun and emotion, students feel the beauty of life and gain hope and courage. However, these days, literature education in middle and high schools is making students perceive literature as difficult and not interesting. By underlining the works and making them memorize them unconditionally, they are making the students less interested in literature. Students should be given ample time to read and think about the work and to express their thoughts. Only then will students be able to fully enjoy literature.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
文学教育的意义在于通过作品传授理解他人、共同生活的智慧，帮助我们理解人生的真谛。学生通过阅读作品，感受其中的乐趣和情感，感受生活的美好，获得希望和勇气。然而，如今，初中和高中的文学教育让学生认为文学是困难的，没有趣味的。通过强调作品并让他们无条件地记住它们，他们使学生对文学的兴趣降低。应该给学生足够的时间来阅读和思考作品并表达他们的想法。只有这样，学生才能充分享受文学。

#######
